Claims emphasize authoritative weight but fail to cite controlling primary authorities (e.g., ECOA/FCRA provisions, Reg B/Reg V, controlling circuit precedent, or specific CFPB/FTC rules), undermining their practical legal force.

Assertions of ‘contradictions’ are not scoped or mapped to concrete provisions—the record lacks identification of which statutes/regulations/cases conflict and which jurisdictions control, so the supposed tensions remain unresolved.

All three claims rely on the same limited source set; source diversity is weak (no binding statutes, no appellate case law, no enforcement orders, no state-law treatment), reducing confidence in legal conclusions.

Mitigation claims (human oversight, disclosures, validation, recordkeeping) are stated as risk-reducing but lack citation to enforcement outcomes or case law showing these measures’ efficacy or limits.
